title: TEN Framework: Voice AI That Can Actually Be Interrupted
summary: TEN Framework is an open-source framework for building real-time voice AI agents that handle streaming audio, interruptions, VAD, turn detection, STT, LLMs, TTS, avatars, telephony, and multimodal workflows. It uses graph-based extensions and real-time audio pipelines to support natural conversational interactions with proper interruption handling and turn detection.
writeup: This Better Stack video covers TEN Framework, an open-source framework for building real-time voice AI agents. It walks through the components that make natural conversation possible: streaming audio pipelines, voice activity detection, turn detection, STT, LLMs, TTS, avatars, telephony, and multimodal workflows. The key takeaway is the architecture: graph-based extensions wired into real-time audio pipelines, with interruption handling and turn detection so an agent can be cut off mid-response rather than talking over the user.
